summary: Iteration 3 (frontend-only scope): verified the two UX iterations — free budget input on Wizard Step 4 + per-category vendor highlights & better unit labels on both consumer PlanView and Admin Results. Backend regression run as a sanity check: pytest 16/16 PASS (no backend changes). All scoped frontend assertions PASS.

minor.issue: Rejected the test fetch payload that embedded a literal '₹' character in the prompt with HTTP 422 — unrelated to this iteration's scope, but flagging in case the prompt validator should accept Unicode currency glyphs. The same prompt with plain text 'INR 4,00,000' or '₹' replaced with 'Rs.' would likely pass.

issues: Tailwind `capitalize` on the h1 still title-cases every word ('A Wedding In Bangalore'). Cosmetic carryover.

action_items: (Optional) Decide whether POST /api/plans should accept '₹' literal in the prompt body (currently 422); easy validator tweak if desired.
(Optional carryover) Replace `capitalize` h1 class in PlanView Headline with explicit first-letter handling.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/lib/utils.js — unitLabel() and vendorHighlights() are clean, side-effect free, well-scoped helpers; both consumer (VendorRow) and admin (VendorCard) reuse them which is exactly the right shape.

Wizard.js inrInWords() is correct for the values tested (525000 → '5.25 lakh', 800000 → '8 lakh', 1500000 → '15 lakh', 1e7 → '1 crore'). Edge case: values like 999999 will render as '10 lakh' due to toFixed rounding — minor cosmetic, low priority.

PlanView.js loads /api/vendors once on mount and memoises into a map — good. Catalog is passed all the way down to VendorRow. Highlights gracefully degrade to null if entry is missing (e.g. catalog still loading) — no crash.
Wizard.js BUDGET_QUICKFILL value-equality match (`budget_inr === q.value`) is correct for the chip's 'selected' visual state.

Admin in-app toast pipeline (5s polling on /api/plans?pending_only=true) still works — verified by a fresh wedding plan created in this run being surfaced as 'NEW APPROVAL REQUEST' and the view button opening the HITL modal.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 additional wedding-in-Bangalore awaiting_approval plan via the wizard (budget ₹8,00,000, 150 guests, 2026-12-18). It remains in the pending queue. The corporate-offsite plan via fetch was NOT created (POST returned 422 because the prompt body contained '₹').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-3 verified UI assertions for budget-input auto-format, budget-quick-{value} chips, vendor-{n}-highlights + per-category unit labels (1-DAY RENTAL, PER GUEST, PACKAGE PRICE, FULL EVENING, COMPLETE PACKAGE) on /plan/:id, and vendor-card-{n}-highlights on /admin. Old budget bucket testids (budget-1L, budget-3L, budget-8L, budget-15L, budget-15L+) have been confirmed REMOVED. Admin arrival-toast → arrival-toast-view → hitl-modal flow still works. Backend untouched in this iter; pytest @ /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py still 16/16. If you need to fire test fetches POST /api/plans from the browser context, prefer 'Rs.' over '₹' (literal ₹ in JSON body causes 422 on the validator).
rca of the issue: n/a — no bugs found in the iteration scope.
